qa_pairs.question: Who automatically has access to a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) award on general-purpose clusters like Béluga, Cedar, and Graham?
qa_pairs.answer: By default, every role sponsored through your Alliance CCDB registration and any co-Principal Investigators (PIs) listed on your RAC application will have access to your RAC award on general-purpose clusters.

qa_pairs.question: What is the process to select specific users for your resource allocation on general-purpose clusters or Niagara?
qa_pairs.answer: To select which users may use your allocation, you need to log in to https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/. From the 'My Account' menu, select 'Manage RAP Memberships'. On the subsequent page, choose the relevant Resource Allocation Project (RAP) from the drop-down list to add members.

qa_pairs.question: How do you add a single new user as a member to a Resource Allocation Project (RAP) for general-purpose clusters or Niagara?
qa_pairs.answer: To add a new member, go to the 'Add Members' section on the 'Manage RAP Memberships' page and enter the user's CCRI. Once added, the new member will be highlighted in yellow.

qa_pairs.question: Can I add multiple sponsored users or co-Principal Investigators (PIs) associated with a new PI to a RAP in bulk for general-purpose clusters or Niagara?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, if you're adding a new PI as a member, you can add any of their sponsored users or co-PIs at the same time. Go to 'Add Members' and click on the 'In bulk' link, which will take you to a new page where you can add several associated roles at once. If the user isn't listed, you can return to the RAP membership page and add them individually by CCRI.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of RAP membership in the context of general-purpose clusters and Niagara?
qa_pairs.answer: RAP membership is represented as a group in LDAP. It defines a group of users who are authorized to submit jobs against the RAPI (the ID of the RAP) and share files within the same Unix group.

qa_pairs.question: How should users specify the account when submitting jobs related to a RAC award on general-purpose clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: When submitting jobs to the scheduler for research described in the RAC application on general-purpose clusters, users need to specify the group name corresponding to the RAC award as the value of the `--account` option, for example, `--account=rrg-profname-ab`.

qa_pairs.question: How should users specify the account when submitting jobs for other research not related to a RAC award on general-purpose clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Jobs pertaining to other research on general-purpose clusters should be submitted with the default group name using the `--account` option, for example, `--account=def-profname`.

qa_pairs.question: What is the recommended tool for transferring substantial amounts of data to an Alliance cluster to use a storage allocation on general-purpose clusters or Niagara?
qa_pairs.answer: For substantial amounts of data to be transferred to an Alliance cluster to use your storage allocation, the use of Globus is strongly recommended.

qa_pairs.question: How is a `/project` storage allocation created on a general-purpose cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: A `/project` storage allocation on a general-purpose cluster is created as a directory of the form `/project/<group-name>`, such as `/project/rrg-profname-ab`, and includes an associated quota for the amount of data that can be stored there.

qa_pairs.question: What kind of files should be stored in the `/project` directory for a RAC application on general-purpose clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Files pertaining to the research described in the RAC application should be stored in the `/project/<group-name>` directory by all sponsored users.

qa_pairs.question: What should be done if data relevant to a RAC application is located in the default `/project` space on a general-purpose cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: If data pertaining to the research described in the RAC application is in your default `/project/def-profname` space, you may wish to move it to the RRG or RPP `/project` directory associated with your RAC award.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find more information about `/nearline` storage on general-purpose clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: For more information about `/nearline` storage on general-purpose clusters, users should consult the 'Using nearline storage' page.

qa_pairs.question: Who has default access to a RAC award on Niagara?
qa_pairs.answer: By default, every role sponsored through your Alliance CCDB registration and any co-Principal Investigators (PIs) listed on your RAC application will have access to your RAC award on Niagara.

qa_pairs.question: How are jobs assigned on Niagara when a user submits them to the scheduler?
qa_pairs.answer: When a user submits a job to the scheduler on Niagara, the job will be assigned to the current allocation of the user's Principal Investigator (PI).

qa_pairs.question: When is it necessary for a user to specify a group name with the `--account` option for jobs on Niagara?
qa_pairs.answer: If a user has more than one Principal Investigator (PI), meaning more than one group, they must specify a group name as the value of the `--account` option when submitting a job on Niagara.

qa_pairs.question: How can the location of a `/project` storage allocation on Niagara be determined?
qa_pairs.answer: The location of a `/project` storage allocation on Niagara should be found by using the environment variable `$PROJECT`. This variable will point to a user-specific directory within the research group's project space.

qa_pairs.question: What does a `/nearline` allocation on Niagara imply?
qa_pairs.answer: A `/nearline` allocation on Niagara means space in HPSS. Further details can be found on the 'Using nearline storage#Niagara' page.
